name: omarchy_system_sleep
content:
System seep 
PCs have ong had a tortured reationship with suspend and hibernation. It seems to be
very difficut for manufacturers to consistenty get these right, athough many do. But
because it's not uniformy supported, Omarchy defauts to ony offering ock, reboot, and
shutdown in the defaut system menu. If you want suspend or hibernation, you' have to
turn it on, and see if it works on your system.
Enabing suspend 
You enabe suspend under Setup > System Seep > Enabe Suspend. That just reveas the
option under System (or Super + Esc ), and then you can see if it works consistenty on
your system. If not, you can turn it off again using Setup > System Seep > Disabe
Suspend.
Enabing hibernation 
You enabe hibernation under Setup > System Seep > Enabe Hibernation. That' create a
/swap subvoume on your boot drive the size of our physica RAM aocation, so make sure
you have penty of room to spare. On a 32GB machine, you' aways need 32GB+ free for
this voume.
Once enabed, you' see the hibernate option under System (or Super + Esc ), and then
you can see if it works consistenty on your system. If not, you can turn it off again using
Setup > System Seep > Disabe Hibernate.
With hibernation turned on, you' aso get suspend-to-hibernate, which migrates the
machine from suspend to hibernate after 30 minutes (as ong as its not connected to
power).
System sleep · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/103/system-sleep
